title: cereus laetus
intro: Cereus laetus, also known as the Saint Anthony's child or St Anthony's child, is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties due to its high concentration of saponins and alkaloids. The health benefits of Cereus laetus include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating various skin conditions such as wounds and burns. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, ointments, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health concerns.
